questions.q: What are the key elements of a safe and effective warm-up? Explain with the help of a suitable example (a) Preparing the muscles, (b) Raising body temperature, (c) Stimulating the nervous system
questions.answer: A safe and effective warm-up is essential for preparing the muscles, raising body temperature, and stimulating the nervous system before engaging in physical activities. The key elements of a warm-up include dynamic stretching, light cardio, and muscle activation exercises. For example, before a basketball game, a player can start with 5-10 minutes of jogging, followed by dynamic stretching such as leg swings, arm circles, and hip rotations. This helps to prepare the muscles for the high-intensity movements involved in the game. Additionally, light cardio exercises such as jumping jacks or burpees can be included to raise body temperature and stimulate the nervous system.
marking_scheme: Explain the key elements of a safe and effective warm-up (2 marks)
Provide a suitable example of a warm-up routine (3 marks)

questions.q: Describe the different types of athletic leadership roles and their characteristics. How does an effective leader facilitate team performance and achievement of goals?
questions.answer: Athletic leadership roles involve guiding and directing team members to achieve common goals. There are two main types of leadership roles: task-oriented and people-oriented. Task-oriented leaders focus on achieving specific goals and objectives, while people-oriented leaders focus on building team morale and motivation. Effective leaders facilitate team performance by setting clear goals, providing positive feedback, and empowering team members to take ownership of their roles. They also create a positive team culture, foster open communication, and make decisions that benefit the team. For example, a basketball coach may be task-oriented, focusing on winning games, while a team captain may be people-oriented, focusing on building team morale and motivation.
marking_scheme: Describe the different types of athletic leadership roles (2 marks)
Explain how an effective leader facilitates team performance (3 marks)

questions.q: Explain the concept of sportsmanship and its importance in competitive sports. Describe three ways in which a player can demonstrate good sportsmanship during a game (a) Respect for opponents, (b) Respect for officials, (c) Respect for teammates and spectators
questions.answer: Sportsmanship is the behavior and attitude that athletes display during and after competition. It involves respecting opponents, officials, teammates, and spectators. Good sportsmanship is essential for maintaining a positive and respectful environment in competitive sports. A player can demonstrate good sportsmanship by respecting opponents, officials, teammates, and spectators in the following ways: (a) Show respect and courtesy to opponents, even in the face of defeat; (b) Listen to and respect the decisions of officials, even if they disagree with them; (c) Support and encourage teammates, and show respect for their efforts and achievements. For example, a tennis player can show respect for their opponent by shaking hands and congratulating them on a good game.
marking_scheme: Explain the concept of sportsmanship (2 marks)
Describe three ways in which a player can demonstrate good sportsmanship (3 marks)

questions.q: Describe the different types of exercise and physical activities that can be included in a fitness program. How can a fitness program be tailored to meet the needs of an individual with a specific fitness goal or objective (a) General fitness, (b) Weight management, (c) Athletic performance
questions.answer: A fitness program can include a variety of exercise and physical activities, such as aerobic exercises, strength training, flexibility exercises, and high-intensity interval training. The type of exercise and physical activity included in a fitness program depends on the individual's fitness goal or objective. For example, a person with a general fitness goal may focus on aerobic exercises such as jogging or cycling, while a person with a weight management goal may focus on strength training and high-intensity interval training. An athlete with a performance goal may focus on specific exercises and drills that improve their speed, agility, and endurance. A fitness program can be tailored to meet the needs of an individual by assessing their current fitness level, setting specific and measurable goals, and creating a program that includes a variety of exercises and physical activities.
marking_scheme: Describe the different types of exercise and physical activities (2 marks)
Explain how a fitness program can be tailored to meet the needs of an individual (3 marks)

questions.q: Explain the role of nutrition in sports performance. Describe three key nutrients that are essential for optimal athletic performance (a) Carbohydrates, (b) Protein, (c) Fats
questions.answer: Nutrition plays a critical role in sports performance, as it provides the energy and nutrients needed to fuel the body during physical activity. Carbohydrates are the primary source of energy for high-intensity, short-duration activities, such as sprinting or jumping. Protein is essential for building and repairing muscle tissue, while fats provide energy for low-intensity, long-duration activities, such as distance running or cycling. Adequate nutrition is also important for maintaining optimal body weight, supporting immune function, and reducing the risk of injury. For example, an athlete may consume a high-carbohydrate meal before a game or competition to provide energy for intense activity.
marking_scheme: Explain the role of nutrition in sports performance (2 marks)
Describe three key nutrients that are essential for optimal athletic performance (3 marks)
